testCases.query: Create a custom button component with CVA variants and OKLCH theming
expectedBehavior: Uses CVA for type-safe variant definitions
Uses cn() utility for class merging without Tailwind conflicts
OKLCH color variables for perceptually uniform theming
Component extension wraps original without modifying source

testCases.query: Build a form with shadcn Input, Label, and validation error states
expectedBehavior: FormField wrapper associates Label with Input via htmlFor
Error state uses aria-invalid and aria-describedby
Uses cn() for conditional error border styling
Integrates with react-hook-form Controller or register

testCases.query: Create a sortable data table with shadcn and TanStack Table
expectedBehavior: Uses TanStack Table useReactTable for state management
Column definitions use accessorKey with header render functions
Sorting toggles via header click with visual indicator
Pagination controls use shadcn Button components

testCases.query: Implement a confirmation dialog for a destructive action
expectedBehavior: Uses AlertDialog (not Dialog) for destructive confirmations
Has Dialog.Title and Dialog.Description for accessibility
Cancel and Action buttons with proper variant styling
Focus trapped within dialog, returns to trigger on close

testCases.query: Create a button that is also a link using asChild composition
expectedBehavior: Uses asChild prop to render Link as the button element
Child component forwards refs with React.forwardRef
Props and event handlers merge correctly via Slot
Renders single element via Slot merging, avoiding nested button-inside-anchor markup

testCases.query: Style a dropdown menu with Tailwind data-attribute variants
expectedBehavior: Uses data-[state=open] for open/close animations on dropdown visibility transitions
Uses data-[side=*] for position-aware slide animations
Focus styles use data-[highlighted] or focus:bg-accent
Disabled items use data-[disabled]:pointer-events-none to prevent interaction

testCases.query: I need to structure our component library using atomic design principles with proper accessibility and variant management patterns
expectedBehavior: Organizes components into atomic design hierarchy of atoms molecules organisms templates and pages
Uses CVA class-variance-authority for type-safe variant definitions with default variants
Meets WCAG 2.1 Level AA contrast requirements with 4.5:1 ratio for normal text
Requires keyboard navigation and proper ARIA attributes for all interactive elements
Uses asChild composition pattern to avoid unnecessary wrapper divs in the DOM

testCases.query: Set up design tokens for colors spacing and typography in our Tailwind project so we can change themes globally without visual drift
expectedBehavior: Defines tokens using W3C Design Token format with structured categories for colors spacing and typography
Uses Tailwind @theme directive with CSS custom properties instead of tailwind.config.js
Applies OKLCH color format for perceptually uniform theming across light and dark modes
Uses semantic token names like bg-primary and text-text-primary instead of hardcoded hex values
Establishes 4px base spacing system with consistent scale from xs through lg

testCases.query: Our components have inconsistent padding and margins everywhere. How do I implement a systematic spacing scale for our design system?
expectedBehavior: Implements 8px grid spacing system with 4px half-step for micro adjustments
Uses Tailwind spacing utilities like p-4 and gap-6 instead of arbitrary pixel values
Applies nested spacing hierarchy with outer containers using larger values than inner elements
Prefers gap and space-y over individual margins for consistent component spacing
Scales spacing responsively with utilities like gap-4 md:gap-6 lg:gap-8

testCases.query: My button component only has a default state. How do I add hover focus disabled and loading states with proper accessibility?
expectedBehavior: Defines all six interactive states: default hover focus active disabled and loading
Uses focus-visible instead of focus to avoid showing ring on mouse click
Implements disabled state with opacity-50 and pointer-events-none for visual feedback
Adds loading state with spinner icon and aria-busy attribute for screen readers
Keeps transition durations under 200ms for responsive interactive feedback

testCases.query: Our headings and body text use arbitrary font sizes with no consistent hierarchy. How do I set up a proper typography scale?
expectedBehavior: Defines semantic typography scale from caption at 12px through page-title at 30px
Pairs font weights intentionally with normal for body medium for labels and semibold for headings
Uses leading-tight for headings and leading-normal for body text line height
Limits project to maximum two font families with one sans-serif plus one monospace

testCases.query: Build a signup form with React Hook Form that has dynamic field arrays for order items and integrates with a date picker component
expectedBehavior: Uses useForm with zodResolver for type-safe validation with Zod schema
Sets validation mode to onBlur for better performance instead of every keystroke
Uses useFieldArray with field.id as key instead of array index for dynamic fields
Wraps third-party components like date pickers with Controller for proper integration
Provides defaultValues to prevent uncontrolled-to-controlled React warnings

testCases.query: I need to validate a contact form on both client and server side using Zod schemas with React 19 Server Actions
expectedBehavior: Shares a single Zod schema between client components and server actions for validation
Uses safeParse instead of parse in server actions to return errors without throwing
Implements useActionState hook for form submission state management in React 19
Supports advanced patterns like discriminated unions for conditional field validation
Uses z.infer for automatic TypeScript type generation from schema definitions

testCases.query: Our team keeps fighting CSS specificity with !important and deeply nested selectors. How do I use cascade layers to fix this?
expectedBehavior: Declares all layers in a single @layer statement at the top of entry CSS file
Orders layers as reset base tokens components utilities and overrides for clear precedence
Assigns third-party CSS to early layers like reset or base for clean style overrides
Eliminates need for !important by restructuring styles into appropriate cascade layers
Notes that unlayered CSS beats all layers and should be kept minimal

testCases.query: We are migrating to Tailwind v4. How do I configure themes and use native container queries without the old config file?
expectedBehavior: Uses @theme directive in CSS for all configuration replacing tailwind.config.js entirely
Applies native container queries with @container and @sm @md @lg variants without plugins
Supports named containers with @container/name syntax for targeted container queries
Uses @max variants for max-width container queries to style below breakpoints

testCases.query: Set up Storybook for our component library with CSF3 stories, interaction testing, and visual regression in CI
expectedBehavior: Uses CSF3 format with satisfies Meta for type-safe story definitions
Creates one story per meaningful visual state including empty loading error and populated
Implements play functions for interaction testing without needing a full E2E framework
Configures Chromatic in CI for automated visual regression detection on every pull request
Adds autodocs tag for automatic documentation generation from component stories
